fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer pharmacist license to washington, pharmacist compact washington, washington pharmacist endorsement application

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Washington.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Pharmacy Program
steps.description: Complete a professional degree program from a recognized school or college of pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Internship Hours
steps.description: Satisfactorily complete the required internship hours under the supervision of a licensed pharmacist.

steps.title: Pass the NAPLEX and MPJE Exams
steps.description: Successfully pass the North American Pharmacist Licensure Exam (NAPLEX) and the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Exam (MPJE) for Washington.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Washington
hero.dek: Washington pharmacist license requires graduation from an accredited pharmacy program, passing the NAPLEX and MPJE exams, and completing internship hours.
